ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class; no specific class for packaged flavoured less-sugar RTD tea. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific facet for 'less sugar' or 'flavoured RTD' variant. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for packaged flavoured less-sugar RTD tea; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle or can of packaged flavoured tea is approximately 250 ml (250 g), providing about 50 kcal and 12.8 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09020032 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged flavoured less-sugar RTD tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with sugar-sweetened tea beverages and dilute sucrose solutions documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). GL per 250 g serving = (55 × 12.8 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 7.0. This is a conservative low-GI estimate; actual GI may vary by flavour and sugar type used.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (5.12 g/100 g) in a liquid vehicle — habitual SSB consumption independently raises blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage due to its added sugar content (5.12 g/100 g), which exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold; switching to unsweetened packaged or brewed tea eliminates this risk while preserving the antioxidant benefit of tea polyphenols.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.12 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre — liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, producing acute postprandial glucose and insulin spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage; the 5.12 g/100 g added sugar with no fibre buffer causes rapid glycaemic excursions, and the 'less sugar' label does not make it safe for regular consumption — unsweetened tea is the recommended alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and increased cardiovascular event risk independent of total caloric intake.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit this beverage; while sodium is low (4 mg/100 g) and fat is absent, the added sugar (5.12 g/100 g) in liquid form contributes to dyslipidaemia and metabolic risk with habitual intake — plain or unsweetened tea is preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (5.12 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; tea polyphenols in the base may offer anti-inflammatory benefit but are offset by the sugar load in this formulation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should prefer unsweetened tea to obtain the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit without the pro-inflammatory effect of added sugar present in this less-sugar variant.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged, Any Flavour) is a commercially produced, ready-to-drink (RTD) tea beverage sold in bottles or cans in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. Known in Chinese as 低糖调味茶（瓶装）and in Malay as Teh berperisa kurang gula, it is made from water, tea extract, added flavourings (such as lemon, peach, jasmine, or other fruit or floral notes), and a reduced quantity of sugar compared with standard sweetened tea drinks. Per 100 g it provides 20 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 5.12 g carbohydrate (entirely as sugar), 0 g dietary fibre, and 4 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the product still contains 5.12 g of added sugar per 100 g — equivalent to approximately 12.8 g per standard 250 ml serving — and carries no fibre to moderate glucose absorption. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to the rapid glycaemic impact of liquid sugar and the independent blood-pressure-raising effect of habitual SSB consumption documented in AHA and WHO guidelines. The tea base may contain polyphenols (catechins, flavonoids) with antioxidant properties, but these benefits are substantially offset by the added sugar. Unsweetened packaged or brewed tea is the recommended alternative for health-conscious consumers. This record is sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient database (cr_id D09020032, confidence 0.95).
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in packaged tea?
Is RTD tea good for hypertension?
Calories in packaged flavoured tea Singapore
Is less sugar tea suitable for weight loss?
What is the GI of packaged tea?
Can I drink flavoured tea if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D09020032, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). This is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the reviewed HPB record.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — this is a tea-based beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — no fat-containing ingredients are present in this formulation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — tea beverages contain no intact dietary fibre. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this packaged flavoured less-sugar tea are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D09020032. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages and dilute sucrose solutions from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. Multilingual local names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been added as this product is distributed and consumed in Singapore. Chinese: 低糖调味茶（瓶装）/ 低糖調味茶（瓶裝）; Malay: Teh berperisa kurang gula (dalam botol); Tamil: குறைந்த சர்க்கரை சுவையூட்டப்பட்ட தேநீர் (பாட்டில்); Hindi: कम चीनी वाली स्वादिष्ट चाय (बोतलबंद). These are functional descriptive translations for a generic commercial product rather than culturally specific dish names.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name is precise and unambiguous. Multilingual local names now present for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for a Singapore-distributed product. Food identity is complete.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (20.0), protein_g (0.0), fat_g (0.0), carbohydrate_g (5.12) all present and numeric. Source is HPB Singapore Foodo — approved primary regional source. Zero macros justified in notes as confirmed zero by HPB source and by product formulation.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (4.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), sugar_g (5.12) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and supported by HPB source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (55), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (7.0), gi_source all present. GI estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 — approved GI source. Estimation basis documented. GI 55 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn tea beverage URI provided with 'closest_parent' match type and gap note. FoodEx2 code A0FJP for tea-based beverages provided with 'closest_parent' match type and gap note. SNOMED CT left null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, how it is produced, multilingual names, clinical relevance for diabetes/hypertension/CVD, and key nutrients. Suitable for RAG and LLM training.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (high), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Human condition name 'Type 2 Diabetes' used correctly. Machine key is type2_diabetes.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 16 entries covering canonical name, alternate English forms, RTD abbreviation, common descriptive variants, and local-language forms in Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Consistency check passed.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types to 'closest_parent'; corrected gi_category to 'low' for GI 55.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory multilingual local names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names to satisfy G6 hard gate. Added corresponding aliases to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language to satisfy Q1 and Q8. Updated notes to document the multilingual additions. Updated natural_language_description to reference multilingual names.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record D09020032 for Flavoured Tea (Less Sugar, Packaged, Any Flavour) now passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The primary fix in iteration 3 was adding Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance and Q1/Q8 alias coverage requirements flagged by the validator.
